task_completion.description: The agent accesses the export or save functionality from the menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'File' menu (or equivalent) in Vital Recorder is open, and an option like 'Export', 'Export to CSV', or 'Save As' is being selected.

task_completion.description: The export or save dialog box is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file dialog box (Save As or Export) is visible on the screen over the Vital Recorder application.

task_completion.description: The export format is set to CSV.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the export/save dialog, the 'Save as type' or format dropdown is set to CSV (*.csv) or Comma Separated Values.

task_completion.description: The correct destination path and filename are entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the current directory as 'Desktop' (or C:\Users\Docker\Desktop) and the 'File name' field contains exactly 'case_0002_export.csv'.

task_completion.description: The exported CSV file is successfully created in the target location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An icon for 'case_0002_export.csv' is visible on the Windows Desktop background, or a File Explorer window open to the Desktop shows the newly created file.

integrity.description: The agent used the Vital Recorder GUI to perform the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Vital Recorder menus and dialogs rather than using command-line tools.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to convert the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No Command Prompt, PowerShell, or code editor windows are open executing scripts to convert the .vital file to .csv.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create a dummy file with the target name.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file creation must follow the Vital Recorder export dialog sequence, not a right-click 'New -> Text Document' on the desktop renamed to .csv.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The loaded file '0002.vital' belongs to a 54-year-old male patient undergoing subtotal gastrectomy (general surgery) at Seoul National University Hospital. The data includes high-resolution waveforms from Primus, Orchestra, Solar 8000M, BIS monitor, and SNUADC devices. The software used is Vital Recorder 1.16.6. If any patient metadata or device tracks are visible in the UI, they should match these details.
